Riyara Trading

Authorised Distributor — All Major Cement, Steel & Aggregate Brands in Tamil Nadu

“Earn Trust with Business”

Building Materials · Chennai, Tamil Nadu · Est. 2014

820 followers·51-200 employees·Building Materials

About

Riyara Trading is one of Tamil Nadu’s leading building material procurement and distribution companies, established in 2014 in Chennai.

CEMENT: Authorised distributor for India Cements (Sankar, Coromandel, Raasi Gold), Chettinad Cement, Penna Cement, Maha Cement, Sagar Cement, Bharathi Cement, and Parashakthi Cement. All grades: OPC 43, OPC 53, PPC, Slag Cement. Bulk delivery via own fleet of 20+ cement bulkers and V-shaped tanker trucks. Multi-state sourcing from Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka.

STEEL: Leading distributor for TATA Steel, Vizag Steel (VSP), and SAIL TMT bars. Sizes 8mm to 32mm. Grades Fe415, Fe500, Fe550.

AGGREGATES: Blue Metal (6-40mm), M-Sand, River Sand, Boulders, GSB. Own network of crushing units and vehicle fleets in Chennai.

One distributor. Every brand. Every grade. Uninterrupted supply since 2014.

LinkedIn page setup: Profile picture = Riyara favicon (red diamond with R). Banner = gradient crimson with RIYARA logo + brand names. Tagline = "Authorised Distributor — All Major Cement, Steel & Aggregate Brands in Tamil Nadu." About section is keyword-rich for search.

Post 3 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Apr 3 · Founder Introduction
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

When people hear “cement trading,” they think it’s simple. Buy here, sell there. But here’s what a typical day looks like: → A contractor in Chennai needs 200 tonnes of OPC 53 by tomorrow → The preferred brand’s plant is on maintenance shutdown → We source from an alternate plant in Andhra Pradesh → Dispatch 8 bulkers overnight → Deliver before the pour begins This is what Riyara does. We’re not manufacturers. We’re the bridge between manufacturers and projects. The people who make sure the right cement, right grade, right quantity reaches the right site at the right time. We’ve been doing this since 2014. Across every major cement brand in Tamil Nadu. #CementDistribution #Logistics #TamilNadu #Business
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

Post 7 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Apr 9 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

After 11 years in this business, I’ve learned that clients don’t choose a distributor for price alone. They choose based on three things: 1. Can you supply ALL the grades I need? (Not just PPC. Sometimes it’s OPC 53 for a flyover, Slag for a marine project.) 2. Can you deliver when I need it? (Not next week. Tomorrow morning.) 3. Can you handle disruptions? (Plant shutdowns happen. Do you have backup sources?) Riyara supplies all grades from all major brands. We have our own fleet. And we source across 4 South Indian states. That’s the whole value proposition. Simple, but hard to replicate. #CementDistribution #SupplyChain #Business #TamilNadu
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

Post 12 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Apr 17 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

It’s not “what’s the price?” It’s: “What happens if the plant shuts down tomorrow?” Because plants DO shut down. Maintenance schedules. Monsoon disruptions. Capacity constraints. A supplier who depends on a single plant is a single point of failure for your project. At Riyara, we source from all major manufacturers across 4 states: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Karnataka. When one source faces a disruption, we activate another. That’s not marketing. That’s 11 years of logistics design. #CementSupply #ProjectManagement #RiskManagement #Construction
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

Post 17 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Apr 24 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

People sometimes ask: “Why don’t you manufacture your own cement?” Because being a distributor IS the value. A manufacturer can only sell their own product. A distributor can offer the client whatever brand, grade, and quantity the project needs. Need India Cements for one project and Chettinad for another? Done. Need OPC 53 from one plant and PPC from another? Done. Need 200 tonnes from 3 different states because one plant is down? Done. That flexibility is what a project needs. Not a brand commitment to one manufacturer. #Distribution #Business #CementIndustry #Flexibility
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

Post 3 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) May 7 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

In the early years, we relied entirely on partner logistics. It worked. Until it didn’t. One monsoon season, third-party trucks were in short supply. Every distributor was competing for the same vehicles. Delivery timelines slipped. Clients were frustrated. That’s when we made the decision: build our own fleet. Today, 20+ owned bulkers and V-shaped tankers means we don’t depend on anyone else for delivery. Our fleet, our schedule, our accountability. It was the most expensive decision we ever made. And the best one. #Logistics #Business #OwnFleet #Entrepreneurship

Post 7 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) May 14 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

In cement, the challenge is logistics — getting 25 tonnes from plant to site on time. In steel, the challenge is inventory. A contractor doesn’t call two weeks in advance. They call when they need 16mm Fe500 from TATA. Today. And they don’t want “whatever you have.” That’s why steel distribution requires deep inventory planning and strong relationships with TATA, VSP, and SAIL. Riyara stocks the range. Because in construction, waiting is not an option. #Steel #Distribution #Business #TMTBars
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

Post 12 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) May 22 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

We didn’t plan to be in the aggregates business. Our cement clients kept asking: “Can you also supply blue metal? M-Sand?” The aggregate market in TN is largely unorganised. Quality varies. Delivery is unreliable. For project managers juggling multiple vendors, it’s a headache. So we built our own network: vetted crushing units, dedicated vehicles, consistent quality. Today, we’re a complete building materials distributor — cement, steel, AND aggregates. Because that’s what our clients needed. #Aggregates #BusinessGrowth #CustomerFirst #BuildingMaterials

Post 17 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) May 29 · Business Insight
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

I never push one brand over another. Because the right brand depends on the project: → High-rise in a coastal area? You need high corrosion resistance. → Mass housing? You need consistent supply at volume. → Infrastructure project? You need specific grades with test certificates. We distribute all 7 major brands. Our job isn’t to sell one brand. It’s to match the right product to the right project. That’s what a good distributor does. #CementDistribution #ClientFirst #Construction #Business

Post 21 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) May 30 · Month Reflection
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

After two months of sharing what Riyara does, I’ve realised something: Clients don’t buy cement. They buy certainty. Clients don’t buy steel. They buy availability. Clients don’t buy aggregates. They buy consistency. The material is the medium. The value is the reliability. Next month, we’ll start sharing some insights about bulk cement logistics, steel grades, and aggregate quality that I think the industry will find useful. Stay tuned. 🙏 #BuildingMaterials #Distribution #Business #TamilNadu

Post 3 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Jun 4 · Education
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

After 11 years of supplying cement, here’s the simplest way I explain grades to clients: OPC 43: Your everyday cement. Residential buildings, plastering, tiling. Reliable and widely available. OPC 53: The high-strength option. Flyovers, bridges, RMC plants, heavy infrastructure. You pay more, but you get more strength. PPC (Portland Pozzolana): The durability pick. Better resistance to moisture and chemicals. Great for coastal and underground structures. Slag Cement: The specialist. Marine projects, chemical plants, and where sulphate resistance matters. Riyara supplies all four. Because different projects need different solutions. #CementGrades #OPC43 #OPC53 #PPC #Construction #RiyaraTrading
👍 Like💬 Comment🔄 Repost📤 Send

riyaratrading Not all aggregates are interchangeable. Each has a purpose. Swipe to learn which one to use where. #Aggregates #BlueMetal #MSand #RiverSand #Construction #RiyaraTrading...
Post 11 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Jun 18 · Education
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

Cement has an expiry date. Most people don’t know this. 0-3 months from manufacture: Full strength. Ideal window. 3-6 months: Slight reduction. Still usable. 6+ months: Significant degradation. Retest before use. This is one reason bulk cement (used immediately upon delivery) often outperforms bags that have been sitting in a warehouse. Freshness matters. Ask your supplier. #CementQuality #Construction #BuildingMaterials #TamilNadu

Post 17 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Jun 26 · Quarter Reflection
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

When we started posting in April, I wasn’t sure what to expect. Three months later, here’s what I’ve learned: 1. The construction industry is hungry for genuine content. Not ads. Real information about materials, grades, logistics. 2. People search for cement brands, not cement distributors. By showing up alongside brands like India Cements, Chettinad, and TATA Steel, we’ve become visible in conversations we weren’t part of before. 3. Educating the market builds trust faster than any sales pitch. 4. The “don’t be promotional” constraint turned into our biggest advantage. By showing what we do instead of claiming what we are, we built credibility. Q3 starts now. Monsoon season. We’ll keep sharing what we see on the ground. #BuildingMaterials #SocialMedia #Business #TamilNadu #Riyara

Post 21 — LinkedIn (Saravanan) Jun 30 · Quarter Reflection
LinkedIn · Saravanan Riyara
SU

Saravanan Udayar

Founder, Riyara Trading

63 posts. 3 months. 4 platforms. What worked: → Brand collab posts performed best — people search for India Cements, Chettinad, TATA, and Riyara shows up alongside them → Fleet content (bulkers, V-shaped tankers) got the most engagement on Instagram → Educational content in June opened new conversations with engineers and project managers What we’re carrying forward: → More brand deep dives (each manufacturer deserves more than one post) → More education (monsoon logistics, grade selection, quality testing) → Client stories (with permission) showing real projects we’ve supplied Monsoon is here. The real test of logistics. We’ll keep sharing. #RiyaraTrading #Q2Wrap #BuildingMaterials #TamilNadu
